24:07 â February 9, 2024
Update to Transferring or Redeeming Bonds Part
We updated information in the Transferring or Redeeming Bonds Part as described in the table below.
Clearing and Deposits Volume
Redeeming or Transferring Bonds Part
Section 3.1, Policy for Redeeming or Transferring Bonds through Central 1
Section 4.8, Preparing Tax Statements
Section 5.2, Determining the Redemption Rate
Section 5.3, Processing Redemptions on Certificate-style Bonds
Section 6.1, Determining the Redemption Rate
We updated the Redemption Policy for the CSB bond type. Most redemption values are now obtained via the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Officeâs online tool. In some situations, youâll need to refer to value tables for redemption values instead.
Section 3.2, Policy for Transporting Negotiable Items to Central 1
We added that Central 1 is unable to accept registered mail. If youâre sending anything to Central 1, we recommend selecting a shipping service where a signature is required.
Section 4.4, Guarantee and Over-Guarantee of Signatures
We added procedures for when bonds registered to a company or charitable organization need to be guaranteed.
Section 4.5, Adding Co-Owner Names and Survivor to Co-Owner on Government of Canada Issued Bonds
We updated references to the Transfer of Ownership, Change of Address, and Estate Transfer (with will or no will) forms.
Section 4.9, Change of Address for Canada Savings Bonds
Section 11.3, Transferring Bonds When Registered Owner is Deceased
We updated some instances of the Canada Savings Bonds Program website to the Bank of Canadaâs Unclaimed Properties Office website, and some form details.
Section 5.3, Processing Redemptions on Certificate-style Bonds
Section 7.2, Processing Redemption
Section 8.2, Processing Coupons
Section 12.3, Settlement and Adjustments for Unmatured Marketable Bonds
We made minor clarifications.
Section 9.2, Preparing the Deposit for Central 1
Section 10.2, Preparing the Deposit for Central 1
We added that, if a deposit is being prepared for Central 1, Canada Savings bonds and Ontario Savings Bonds must be part of separate Canadian deposits.
